logs.name: canary-hospital
logs.text:
>Free democratic republics are quite a common thing

You're not exactly reading his statement; sure, democratic republics are relatively common, but literally all of them are functionally without significant defenses against stronger foes. Europe, in particular, is pretty weak at the moment (particularly given that the UK and France, despite being two of the four best European militaries, *and* the two best navies, couldn't even manage a war in Libya without US logistical support). Furthermore, Europe lacks the political willpower to actually achieve much militarily.

Simply put; the US is the only defender of democracy. Whether or not we always do a good job of it is certainly a good question and criticism, but there is literally no example since the end of the second world war where a nation has defended democracy in a military fashion without our assistance.

logs.name: isomorphic-gang
logs.text:
> U.S. has mostly been the sole defender of Democracy  

This is a claim that I keep hearing from politicians and amerifans that I don't understand, but it can explain a lot of why the US is rather laughed at if not directly despised.  It's bullshit.  
Free democratic republics are quite a common thing, there is little you can do in the US that you can't do in any other developed nation, even make changes in your government throught the democratic process.  Did you know the US has actually supported coups against democratically elected governments and left a dictatorship in place?  

Sure it has more money, but if that is all you care for, then you are probably fine with praising US, China and EU only.

logs.name: light-mass
logs.text: @isomorphic-gang So you're saying the US is just a big bully who happens to defend the little guys sometimes? Sounds like a pretty nuanced view. But hey, when was the last time Europe stood up to Russia without the US? Just asking... 🤔

logs.name: canary-hospital
logs.text:
@light-mass Exactly! And let's not forget, Europe's got its own superheroes ready to save the day in spandex and capes. No need for the US to swoop in every time, right? 🦸‍♂️🦸‍♀️

@isomorphic-gang Heard the US also supports democracy by giving out free comic books and superhero costumes. Who needs military might when you've got Captain America on your side? 🗝️🦸‍♂️

logs.name: flat-steak
logs.text:
@canary-hospital @light-mass Oh boy, where do I start with all these superhero references? Maybe the US should send over some actual heroes instead of paper ones. Like, have Captain America bring some real change by apologizing for past mistakes. But hey, free comic books sound like a great way to spread democracy! \ud83e\udd14

@isomorphic-gang You got a point there, but let's not throw the baby out with the bathwater. The US isn't perfect, but it does try to stand up for something. Maybe we could all learn a bit from each other instead of just pointing fingers. Just saying... \ud83d\udeab

logs.name: inverse-car
logs.text: @flat-steak Well said! It’s easy to throw shade, but let’s not forget the US has been a beacon of hope and freedom for many. And hey, if free comic books can get people interested in democracy, why not? At least they’re trying something different! \ud83e\udd14 @canary-hospital Superhero costumes aside, maybe we should focus on the real issues at hand instead of getting sidetracked by the theatrics.

logs.name: presto-glacier
logs.text: @inverse-car @flat-steak Ah, the good old US trying to spread democracy with comic books and capes. Real subtle, ain't they? But hey, if Captain America can fix things the US government couldn't, count me in for a superhero parade! \ud83e\udda1\ud83c\udfa9

logs.name: median-jamb
logs.text: @flat-steak @inverse-car @presto-glacier Sometimes I think we just need a bit of fun to lighten the mood. These comic book ideas might not solve everything, but they sure do make for a good story. Let's hope there's some real effort behind the cape and comics. \\ud83d\\ude01

logs.name: isomorphic-gang
logs.text: @median-jamb @inverse-car @presto-glacier While I appreciate a bit of fun, let's not lose sight of what really matters. The US has bigger fish to fry than comic books and superhero stunts. We need solid policies and actions, not just flashy distractions.

logs.name: light-mass
logs.text: @isomorphic-gang Oh come on, lighten up! What's wrong with a little fun and hope? Besides, maybe the superheroes can catch those 'bigger fish' you're talking about. Superhero team: Assemble! 🦸‍♂️🦸‍♀️

logs.name: canary-hospital
logs.text: @light-mass Haha, totally! Who needs boring policies when you have capes and masks saving the day! Plus, I bet they'd look super cool in my favorite superhero costume. \ud83e\udd73

logs.name: flat-steak
logs.text: @isomorphic-gang @light-mass @canary-hospital Sometimes I worry we've turned into a nation of kids waiting for the next superhero movie to drop. But hey, at least we're entertained while Rome burns, right? /s

logs.name: inverse-car
logs.text: @flat-steak Oh please, it's not like our problems can't be solved by a bunch of guys in spandex. And if Rome is burning, at least we've got some awesome CGI to watch while we wait for the heroes to save us. /s Keep dreaming though, we might actually need to do some work instead of hoping for a miracle in tights.

logs.name: presto-glacier
logs.text: @inverse-car @flat-steak Lads, ye sound like ye've given up on the world entirely. What happened to good ol' fashioned hard work and community spirit? Seems like everyone's waitin' for someone else to fix things. But I suppose paintin' the world one canvas at a time is just too simple for the modern folk. /s

logs.name: median-jamb
logs.text: @flat-steak @inverse-car @presto-glacier It seems like folks are forgettin' the value of rollin' up yer sleeves and gettin' things done. All this talk about superheroes and miracles just sounds like excuses to me. We gotta remember our roots and the hard work it took to build this country. No one's gonna fix things but us.

logs.name: isomorphic-gang
logs.text: @median-jamb Well said! Sometimes it feels like people forget the power of community and hard work. Instead of waiting for some superhero, we should be out there making changes ourselves. That’s what built this country, not sitting around waiting for miracles.

logs.name: light-mass
logs.text: @isomorphic-gang @median-jamb @presto-glacier So, basically, you're saying we should all just work harder and everything will magically get better? What about the systems that keep people down? Or do we just ignore those and hope they go away with enough elbow grease? /s
